mission.title: keycloak: Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs
mission.description: Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs. This issue affects 77+ users.

steps.title: Identify keycloak troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your keycloak deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak -n keycloak --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review keycloak configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant keycloak configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak
kubectl get configmap -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keycloak
```
### Describe the bug

Following this [discussion](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/10937), we are experience the problem.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs
steps.description:
>  If each tab does its own authentication request, wouldn't that then result in different client sessions? Or, do we end up with a single client session for multiple tabs? Reason I'm asking is that sharing a refresh token as suggested here through sessionStorage is effectively resulting in actual invalid re-use of a refresh token, while if a refresh token is not stored in sessionStorage and each that does an authentication/authorization request on their own they would then have different

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak -n keycloak --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n keycloak --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with keycloak installed or the issue environment reproducible.
